snippets.title: End-to-end conflict resolution
snippets.code:
git switch main && git pull --ff-only
git switch feature/auth
git merge main                          # or: git rebase main

# Git pauses with conflicts:
git status
#   both modified:   src/config.py
#   both modified:   prompts/qa.md

# Open each file, replace the marker blocks with the correct combined content.
# Then:
git add src/config.py prompts/qa.md
git commit                                # for merge
# or:
git rebase --continue                     # for rebase

# Bail out if you change your mind:
git merge --abort
# or:
git rebase --abort
snippets.explanation: Same shape every time — status, edit, add, continue (or abort).

snippets.title: Turn on diff3 style for richer markers
snippets.code:
git config --global merge.conflictStyle diff3

# Now your markers include the common ancestor:
# <<<<<<< HEAD
#     timeout = 30
# ||||||| common ancestor
#     timeout = 10
# =======
#     timeout = 60
# >>>>>>> feature/slow-llm
snippets.explanation: Showing the ancestor lets you see what *both* sides changed *from* — usually the deciding piece of context.

snippets.title: Pick one side wholesale when intent is clear
snippets.code:
# Keep MY version of a conflicted file:
git checkout --ours  path/to/file.py
git add path/to/file.py

# Keep THEIR version:
git checkout --theirs path/to/file.py
git add path/to/file.py

# (During rebase, --ours and --theirs are swapped vs your intuition because
#  the 'replaying' branch sits in the 'incoming' slot. Read the docs!)
snippets.explanation: Useful when the entire file's worth of changes from one side is correct and the other side is obsolete.

snippets.title: Mergetool — use VS Code (or any 3-way differ)
snippets.code:
# One-time config:
git config --global merge.tool vscode
git config --global mergetool.vscode.cmd 'code --wait $MERGED'

# When conflicts happen:
git mergetool
snippets.explanation: Opens each conflicted file in your preferred 3-way diff editor; saving + closing marks the file resolved.

snippets.title: Reuse recorded resolutions (`rerere`)
snippets.code:
git config --global rerere.enabled true

# After this, every conflict you resolve is recorded.
# If you hit the SAME conflict again (e.g. during a re-rebase), Git auto-applies your previous resolution.
snippets.explanation: Lifesaver during painful rebases that touch the same lines repeatedly.

snippets.title: Notebook conflicts with `nbdime`
snippets.code:
pip install nbdime
nbdime config-git --enable --global    # plug nbdime into git diff/merge

# Now `git diff notebook.ipynb` shows a semantic diff,
# and `git mergetool` opens nbdime's web UI for notebook conflicts.
snippets.explanation: JSON conflict markers in a notebook are unreadable; nbdime shows cell-level semantic differences instead.
